item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first three paragraphs are so thick with fake jargon that I nearly downvoted for that alone. Belial is the most interesting character, which isn't really saying much considering how little dialogue he gets, then dies in the second log. Dankworth's commentary in the incident logs is overdramatic and his name is so goofy it keeps draining my suspension of disbelief. Plus there's so many Big Things happening in this article — Lincoln being fake, the hamburger having a different name — that they all just bounced off me instead of making me do a double-take. All of this is in service of a punchline, which, again, could potentially be cool but gets neutered by how overdramatically it's stated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For context, I just got done binging SCP-3125 and all the related tales. So I can't help but compare a bit.

To me, this reads a bit technically overwrought — too much jargon, too convoluted and technical. I like the idea, but getting through it felt like a chore, at times. And, because of the dense technical wording, I just didn't really get it. What happened to jellies? I still have no idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good shit.

Edit: You also used my Task Force! I'd upvote twice if I could :D

item.title: Re: Good shit, +1
item.content:encoded:
Both points are correct, and I will fix them immediately.

I really appreciate how much I was able to work with this idea; I love how what was a one-off, strange line works itself into a centerpiece in the rest of the article.

Thank you!

item.title: Good shit, +1
item.content:encoded:
I really like what you did with the containment procedures I sent over, and love the lore additions. I would say this even if I wasn't the coauthor

two things I noticed tho:
"The process by which SCP-5712 associates concepts can be controlled through interactions with SCP-5712 in the form of word association games."

Contextually, it sounds like you meant to say the following, but I could be wrong
"The process by which SCP-5712 associates concepts can be controlled through interactions with SCP-5712-A in the form of word association games."

Also, I'm pretty sure you meant to link 5800 instead of 5880. I'll fix the latter but since I'm not completely sure on the first one I'll leave it be

item.title: f1rst
item.content:encoded: You published it, congratulations! +1, was good in review and remains good on the site. :D

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
That's my corpsecon, I guess. Somehow.

Thanks to anyone who helped with crit, including Naveil, nokko, and Veralta, and a few more that I can't remember. This was one of those articles where I wasn't really sure at what point it was good enough, and you guys really made me feel more comfortable with what I had.

Images:

  • Peirce
  • Belial Sigil
  • BELIAL component removal

First two are from Wikimedia Commons, last is by NASA and therefore public domain.

Thanks for reading!
